source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 Class 1 CV vehicle Impact Attenuator (IA) as a multi-part assembly per FS Rules 2026 T.3.20 / T.3.21, explicitly modeling the IA as the physical collection of separately-manufactured components that are bonded and bolted together to form the crash-energy-absorbing structure in front of the front bulkhead. The IA must decelerate a 300 kg mass moving at 7.0 m/s with mean deceleration <= 20g (196 m/s^2) and peak deceleration <= 40g (392 m/s^2), absorbing at least 7350 J of kinetic energy. The assembly consists of four part families: (A) the 5052-H32 aluminum outer shell (4 mm nominal wall) that defines the forward aerodynamic profile and provides lateral restraint of the honeycomb core; (B) the 5052/3003 aluminum honeycomb core blocks at 83 kg/m^3 density with 2.35 MPa plateau crush strength, stacked and bonded inside the outer shell to provide the bulk of the kinetic-energy absorption through progressive crushing; (C) the 8 mm 5052-H32 anti-intrusion plate (AIP) bolted behind the honeycomb/shell stack to the front bulkhead of the chassis, providing the final intrusion barrier after the core has densified; (D) the 8 grade-8 M8 steel fasteners (nuts and bolts) that join the AIP to the chassis front bulkhead on a 100 x 80 mm rectangular pattern per the original AIP bolt schedule.

Geometry inherits the original sample: IA forward-aft 300 mm (>= 200 mm minimum per FS T.3.20.2) x 250 mm wide x 200 mm tall (>= 200 mm x 100 mm minimum per FS T.3.20.2). Outer shell wall thickness 4 mm. Honeycomb core fills the internal volume (approximately 14 L) and is sized to give a 200 mm crush stroke matching the bounding envelope forward dimension. AIP: 300 mm wide x 200 mm tall x 8 mm thick with 2 rows of 4 M8 (9 mm clearance) holes on a 100 x 80 mm rectangular pattern. Fasteners: 8 grade-8 steel M8 bolts (length selected by the team to match the bulkhead-to-AIP stack thickness) with matching nuts and washers. Overall bounding envelope 320 x 250 x 212 mm (AIP overhangs shell and AIP plate is 12 mm thick counting washers).

Materials inherit verbatim from the original: outer shell and AIP are 5052-H32 aluminum (E 70.3 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2680 kg/m^3, yield 193 MPa, UTS 228 MPa); honeycomb core is 3003 aluminum Al5052-H19 equivalent with 2.35 MPa plateau crush stress at 83 kg/m^3 density and 70% densification strain; fasteners are grade-8 steel (team-selected within the FS Rules fastener allowance). Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 head-on 300 kg at 7.0 m/s with target KE absorption >= 7500 J, mean decel target 18g (buffer below 20g rule), peak decel target 35g (buffer below 40g rule); LC2 oblique 10-deg off centerline at the same 300 kg and 7.0 m/s; LC3 quasi-static pre-test at 40 kN compressive force uniformly distributed on the forward face.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) under LC1, mean deceleration <= 20g AND peak deceleration <= 40g AND absorbed energy >= 7350 J; (R2) under LC2 oblique, peak deceleration <= 40g AND absorbed energy >= 7350 J; (R3) under LC3 quasi-static, peak von Mises in the AIP plate body must not exceed 129 MPa and no outer-shell wall buckling may occur before 2 kN of the quasi-static load; (R4) outer-shell linear buckling eigenvalue must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 0.8 (progressive-collapse intent: buckling is the intended mode but must not precede 80% of crush-initiation); (R5) IA + AIP + fastener total mass must not exceed 4.0 kg; (R6) AIP must be continuous and must not protrude past the bulkhead envelope. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: assembly-level energy absorption, assembly-level peak deceleration, and connection DCR (AIP-to-bulkhead bolt shear + bearing + tearout) must all be reported; individual part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Thermal, fluid, vibration, and radiation analyses are not required. Explicit-dynamic crash simulation (LC1, LC2) is the primary solver; linear static (LC3) and linear buckling (outer shell under LC1 initial crush) supplement. Deliver STEP AP242 and Parasolid of the assembly with each body (outer shell, honeycomb core block(s), AIP, each fastener) as a distinct solid, tagged with part_family (A|B|C|D) and instance_index, plus a crash report giving mean decel, peak decel, absorbed energy, AIP peak stress, outer-shell buckling factor, fastener DCR, and total assembly mass.
short_prompt:
Design a Formula Bharat impact attenuator + anti-intrusion plate as a multi-part assembly per FS Rules 2026 T.3.20/T.3.21. Envelope 300 x 250 x 200 mm; total mass <= 4.0 kg. Core AA 5052-H32 honeycomb; front AIP plate per regulations.

Load cases: LC1 dynamic crash 300 kg vehicle at 7.0 m/s (mean deceleration <= 20g, peak <= 40g, energy absorbed >= 7350 J); LC2 quasi-static proof 40 kN front load without failure.

Pass/fail: LC1 energy and g-limits met; LC2 no AIP yield; FEM at assembled level; bolt-pattern DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: IA crash event is a transient large-deformation problem with honeycomb plateau crushing; requires explicit-dynamic solver with material failure, not linear static FEA.

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA per FS T.3.20 / T.3.21 (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part FS 2026 IA assembly: 5052-H32 outer shell (A) + aluminum honeycomb core block(s) (B) + 8 mm AIP plate (C) + 8x M8 grade-8 fasteners (D). Envelope 300 forward x 250 x 200 with AIP 300x200x8; crash decel <= 20g mean / 40g peak; absorb >= 7350 J; assembly-level verification with per-part diagnostic metrics.

parts.name: outer_shell_5052
parts.role: formed 4 mm 5052-H32 outer shell that confines the honeycomb core laterally and sets the forward aerodynamic profile; progressively collapses along with the core during the crash event

parts.name: honeycomb_core_block
parts.role: bonded aluminum-honeycomb core blocks (3003 Al5052-H19-equivalent at 83 kg/m^3, 2.35 MPa plateau) that absorb the bulk of the kinetic energy by progressive crushing along the forward-aft axis
parts.count_note: Single block is sufficient; teams often stack 2-3 blocks of increasing density for tuned decel pulses; modeled here as 1-3 blocks.

parts.name: anti_intrusion_plate_aip
parts.role: 8 mm 5052-H32 anti-intrusion plate bolted to the front bulkhead; final barrier after honeycomb densification; also serves as the mounting flange for the IA to the chassis

parts.name: aip_fastener
parts.role: M8 grade-8 steel bolt + nut + washer assemblies that connect the AIP to the front bulkhead chassis tube/plate on a 2x4 pattern

geometric_constraints.bolt_grade: grade-8 steel (team-selected within FS fastener allowance)

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 320 x 250 x 212 mm including AIP overhang
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: vehicle coordinates: +x forward, +y starboard, +z up; IA origin at AIP rear face center

boundary_conditions.aip_to_bulkhead: 8x M8 grade-8 bolted connections: modeled as tied fastener pairs with axial and shear stiffness matching the commercial M8 grade-8 bolt in single shear through an 8 mm AIP and team-selected bulkhead plate
boundary_conditions.honeycomb_to_shell: bonded contact (epoxy film) along the inner periphery of the outer shell; modeled as tied surfaces
boundary_conditions.shell_to_aip: bonded lap flange around the AIP rear perimeter; modeled as tied surface
boundary_conditions.crash_impact_interface: 300 kg rigid mass striking the forward face at 7.0 m/s (LC1, LC2) or 40 kN quasi-static distributed pressure on forward face (LC3)

prompt.objective: primary: pass all five R-criteria under assembled_only FEM check. secondary: minimize assembly mass below 4.0 kg.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: grade-8 M8 bolt shear + bearing + tearout per team-selected bolt capacity table

interfaces.load_path: lateral confinement of honeycomb core by the outer shell during crush; transfers hoop / lateral membrane stress
rear-flange bond transferring residual axial load and the final intrusion load from the shell into the AIP
honeycomb core rear face bonded to AIP forward face; transfers densified-core axial reaction
M8 grade-8 bolts transmit axial crush reaction from the AIP into the chassis front bulkhead

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: An IA is physically manufactured as a layered/bonded assembly of an outer skin, honeycomb core, AIP, and bolted attachment to the chassis - not a monolithic part.
notes.speculation_caveats: part A count (shell may be single drawn part or 5-piece welded box), part B count (1-3 stacked core blocks with different densities), and exact bolt/washer stack thicknesses are speculated; the inner-volume / crush-stroke geometry follows the original sample.
